name: Optimize wjson performance across Lua versions

description: New baseline performance after bench.lua modification

asi.notes: New baseline after updating bench.lua to clear JIT traces and randomize dataset order.

description: Optimize escape_string (LuaJIT) by removing dual-pass scanning. -8% encode_ms.

asi.hypothesis: Remove redundant scan pass in escape_string (LuaJIT)
asi.key_insight: The previous logic scanned the string once to count escapes and then again to build. Removing the first pass simplifies the code and reduces overhead.
asi.next_action: Try inlining more hot paths or optimizing table usage.

description: Use multi-argument str_byte for literals and unicode escapes. -3% decode on PUC Lua.

asi.hypothesis: Use multi-argument str_byte to reduce function calls for fixed-length tokens (true, false, null, \uXXXX)
asi.result: Consistent improvement across JIT and PUC Lua 5.3+.
asi.key_insight: str_byte(str, i, j) is significantly faster than multiple single-byte calls as it avoids repeated function overhead. This is especially helpful for literals and hex parsing.
asi.next_action: Try reducing allocations in parse_object or optimizing array metatable access.

description: Structural peeking in parse_array and parse_object. -4% on Lua 5.4.

asi.hypothesis: Avoid skip_whitespace in compact JSON by peeking for structural characters (comma, bracket, brace, colon)
asi.result: Significant win for PUC Lua (especially 5.4), neutral for LuaJIT.
asi.key_insight: In compact JSON, structural characters are usually at the next byte. Peeking avoids the entry/exit overhead of the skip_whitespace scanner.
asi.next_action: Explore object key interning or optimizing table re-allocations.

description: Positive integer fast-path in decode_value. Small overall win.

asi.hypothesis: Inline non-zero positive integer parsing to avoid function calls for common numeric tokens
asi.result: Small but consistent win across all versions.
asi.key_insight: Positive integers starting with 1-9 are the most common number tokens. Inlining the accumulator avoids the setup/teardown of parse_number.
asi.next_action: Look into improving table iterations in encode_value.

description: Escaped Key Cache for objects. Consistent wins across PUC Lua.

asi.hypothesis: Cache escaped representation of frequently repeated object keys.
asi.result: Significant win for all PUC Lua versions (up to 3.6%), neutral for LuaJIT total (but -2.6% on encode).
asi.key_insight: In many datasets, keys are repeated thousands of times. Caching avoids redundant scanning and str_gsub calls.
asi.next_action: Try inlining decode_value dispatch for most common types (string/number).

description: Separated JIT/PUC hot paths. Removed decode_value bloat. Flattened object encoder.

asi.hypothesis: Reducing code complexity in LuaJIT-critical functions (decode_value) and separating JIT/PUC paths will improve JIT performance.
asi.result: LuaJIT improved by 2.4% over baseline. PUC Lua remained stable.
asi.key_insight: Excessive branching and complex logic in the hottest trace roots (decode_value) can inhibit LuaJIT optimizations even if the code looks faster.
asi.next_action: Try Tier 1 #3: Reduce parse_number slow-path re-scanning.
